reasoning: The market price on May 15, 2025, was 0.09. However, the search results strongly indicate that 'Operation Rough Rider', a major U.S. bombing campaign against the Houthis in Yemen, started in mid-March 2025 and concluded with a ceasefire mediated by Oman around May 6, 2025. Throughout this intense period of military engagement, the U.S. relied entirely on airstrikes and did not deploy regular ground troops, with analysts repeatedly affirming that doing so is 'highly unlikely.' The U.S. government policy appears well-aligned against putting regular ground combat troops in Yemen; instead, they operate through local militias, contractors, military advisors, and special operations forces (SOF), all of which are explicitly excluded by the resolution criteria. The fact that the peak of the recent escalation (mid-March to early May) ended without regular ground troops demonstrates extreme reluctance by the U.S. to commit ground forces. Given the May 6 ceasefire and the strict criteria (regular military personnel physically entering Yemen), the probability is very low, lower than the mid-May market price, as the ceasefire seems to have held and the window is closing. I set the probability to 4%.
